channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Fri, 15 May 2026 23:06:00 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Meningitis outbreak in Berkshire causes panic in three schools
item.description: A student's death from meningitis at Henley College has prompted health officials to investigate links to Reading Blue Coat and Highdown Secondary schools. While the Berkshire strain differs from the Kent outbreak, authorities are providing precautionary treatment to close contacts. Experts emphasize the disease requires close contact to spread, with wider public risk remaining low.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Michelle Obama: “When someone is cruel or acts like a bully, you don’t stoop to their level. No, our motto is…”
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 50 Green Card holders identified to be deported as US starts re-vetting older cases
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Carla Nelson ends legislative career after years of service in Minnesota Senate
item.description: Minnesota State Senator Carla Nelson announced her retirement after serving Olmsted and Dodge counties since 2010. A former teacher, Nelson championed initiatives like the Destination Medical Center and secured significant funding for K-12 education. Her legislative career, marked by achievements in education, healthcare, and economic growth, concludes with her term's end, opening the door for new representation.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Did it as a favour to Pakistan': Trump on Iran ceasefire
item.description: President Trump stated the US agreed to a ceasefire with Iran as a favor to Pakistan, accepting the truce at Islamabad's request. This comes as Pakistan seeks to mediate the US-Iran crisis, despite reports suggesting it may have provided logistical support to Iran, raising questions about its neutrality.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'FBI has not forgotten': Who is ex-Air Force officer Monica Witt who fled to Iran after spying?
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Polish EU lawmaker threatens legal action against Starmer over ‘hatred and division’ remarks on London rally
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'I could tell them I don't want any students but...': Trump on accepting 500,000 Chinese students in US
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Not Our War: Behind closed doors, UAE urged Gulf action against Iran, Saudis refused - report
item.description: The UAE sought a joint military response from Gulf nations against Iran after Tehran's attacks, but Saudi Arabia and Qatar refused, citing it as "not their war." This disagreement strained UAE ties with its neighbors and contributed to Abu Dhabi's growing frustration with regional partners.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UK student dies of Meningitis as expert warns disease can be ‘very lethal’
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Only 2.5 years sentence for Sukhwinder Sidhu, truck driver who crashed and killed ex-Olympian in Canada
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Trump wanted to go nuclear on Iran but ...': Ex-CIA analyst makes big claim
item.description: Former CIA analyst Larry Johnson claims President Trump considered a nuclear strike on Iran. He alleges Chairman of the Joint Chiefs General Dan Caine firmly rejected the idea, preventing its execution. Johnson explained the military chain of command and the potential for Caine's resignation if overruled, adding context to Trump's subsequent public statements against nuclear use.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Yes, I did it: 22-year-old Indian pleads guilty to smuggling people across US-Canada border; $100 per head
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Fact check: Did Chud The Builder post racist Facebook updates after courthouse shooting? Viral screenshot debunked
item.description: Streamer Dalton “ChudTheBuilder” Eatherly was arrested after a courthouse shooting in Clarksville, Tennessee. Soon after, a viral screenshot claimed he posted racist comments on Facebook about the incident. However, there is no verified evidence the Facebook post was real. Authorities charged Eatherly with attempted murder and other firearm-related offenses following the shooting investigation.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: Indian-origin man convicted of attempted murder of woman and child in London stabbing
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Received messages': Iran FM Araghchi says US wants to continue peace talks
item.description: Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araghchi claimeed that Tehran has received new messages from the US expressing a desire to continue talks, despite earlier public rejections. Araghchi stated that while Iran lacks trust in American intentions, they are working to preserve a fragile ceasefire to allow diplomacy a chance, emphasizing that military pressure has proven ineffective.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Amelia Earhart’s 90-year disappearance mystery took a haunting turn: What scientists found after years of analysis shocked researchers
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The $100 million hospital gift by Marc and Lynne Benioff reshaped local health care in Hawaii
item.description: Hawaii Pacific Health received a record $100 million donation. This significant gift will fund the redevelopment of the Straub Benioff Medical Centre in Honolulu. The project aims to increase patient capacity and modernize facilities. This investment will create a future healthcare campus for Hawaii. The donation focuses on tangible improvements for community health.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Leonard Lauder: The man turned a private Cubist collection into public history
item.description: Leonard A. Lauder's monumental gift of his Cubist art collection to the Metropolitan Museum of Art in 2013 transformed private treasures into public assets. This significant donation, featuring masterpieces by Picasso and Braque, democratized access to art history and bolstered the Met's modern art offerings. The accompanying research center further cemented its lasting impact.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Imam, 54, who claimed ‘jinn powers’ jailed for life over abuse crimes against women and girls
item.description: A former East London imam, Abdul Halim Khan, has been jailed for life after being convicted of 21 sexual offences, including rape and child rape, against seven victims over a decade. He exploited his religious authority, claiming supernatural powers to manipulate and abuse vulnerable women and children. The case highlights safeguarding failures and barriers victims face when reporting abuse.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
